annotations.label: Vector space

anchor.snippet: A vector space over a field F is a non-empty set V together with a binary operation and a binary function that satisfy the eight axioms listed below

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Module R M` over a field R is exactly a vector space; it generalizes from field to semiring.

annotations.label: Vector addition

anchor.snippet: The binary operation, called vector addition or simply addition assigns to any two vectors

annotations.note: Module extends `AddCommMonoid`/`AddCommGroup`, providing the vector addition operation via `HAdd.hAdd`.

annotations.label: Scalar multiplication

anchor.snippet: The binary function, called scalar multiplication , assigns to any scalar

annotations.note: Scalar multiplication is the `SMul` class, with `Module` packaging the axiomatic compatibility with addition.

annotations.label: Real and complex vector spaces

anchor.snippet: When the scalar field is the real numbers , the vector space is called a real vector space

annotations.note: Real/complex vector spaces are `Module ℝ V` / `Module ℂ V` instances of the generic `Module` class.

annotations.label: Vector space as module over a field

anchor.snippet: Even more concisely, a vector space is a module over a field

annotations.note: Mathlib defines vector spaces precisely this way: `Module K V` with `K` a field.

annotations.label: Linear combination

anchor.snippet: a linear combination of elements of G is an element of V of the form

annotations.note: `Finsupp.linearCombination` sends a finitely supported coefficient family to its linear combination.

anchors.snippet: a linear combination of elements of G is an element of V of the form

annotations.label: Linearly independent

anchor.snippet: are said to be linearly independent if no element of G can be written as a linear combination of the other elements of G

annotations.note: `LinearIndependent R v` is defined as injectivity of `Finsupp.linearCombination`.

anchors.snippet: are said to be linearly independent if no element of G can be written as a linear combination of the other elements of G

annotations.label: Linear subspace

anchor.snippet: A linear subspace or vector subspace W of a vector space V is a non-empty subset of V that is closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication

annotations.note: `Submodule R M` is a subset closed under addition, zero, and scalar multiplication; it specialises to a linear subspace when `R` is a field.

anchors.snippet: A linear subspace or vector subspace W of a vector space V is a non-empty subset of V that is closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication

annotations.label: Linear span

anchor.snippet: the linear span or simply the span of G is the smallest linear subspace of V that contains G

annotations.note: `Submodule.span R s` is the smallest submodule containing `s`.

anchors.snippet: the linear span or simply the span of G is the smallest linear subspace of V that contains G

annotations.label: Basis of a vector space

anchor.snippet: A subset of a vector space is a basis if its elements are linearly independent and span the vector space

annotations.note: `Basis` is the structure bundling a linearly independent, spanning family, defined as a `LinearEquiv` with `ι →₀ R`.

anchors.snippet: A subset of a vector space is a basis if its elements are linearly independent and span the vector space

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis

anchor.snippet: Every vector space has at least one basis, or many in general

annotations.note: `exists_basis : ∃ s : Set V, Nonempty (Basis s K V)` proves every vector space has a basis.

anchors.snippet: Every vector space has at least one basis, or many in general

annotations.label: Dimension theorem for vector spaces

anchor.snippet: all bases of a vector space have the same cardinality , which is called the dimension of the vector space

annotations.note: `mk_eq_mk_of_basis` states any two bases have the same cardinality (over a ring satisfying `StrongRankCondition`).

anchors.snippet: all bases of a vector space have the same cardinality , which is called the dimension of the vector space

annotations.label: Coordinates on a basis

anchor.snippet: are called the coordinates of v on the basis

annotations.note: `Basis.repr` is the linear equivalence sending `v` to its coordinates `ι →₀ R`.

anchors.snippet: are called the coordinates of v on the basis

annotations.label: Coordinate map is an isomorphism

anchor.snippet: It is thus a vector space isomorphism , which allows translating reasonings and computations

annotations.note: `Basis.repr` is by definition a `LinearEquiv` between `M` and `ι →₀ R`, hence an isomorphism.

annotations.label: Arrows in the plane

anchor.snippet: The first example of a vector space consists of arrows in a fixed plane

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal counterpart to the informal arrows-in-the-plane example as a named declaration.

annotations.label: Ordered pairs of numbers

anchor.snippet: A second key example of a vector space is provided by pairs of real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib supplies `Module` instances on products like `ℝ × ℝ` via `Prod.module`.

anchors.snippet: A second key example of a vector space is provided by pairs of real numbers

annotations.label: Coordinate space F^n

anchor.snippet: The simplest example of a vector space over a field F is the field F itself

annotations.note: `Pi.module` provides a `Module` instance on `ι → F`, realizing the coordinate space `Fⁿ`.

anchors.snippet: The simplest example of a vector space over a field F is the field F itself

annotations.label: Complex numbers and field extensions

anchor.section: Complex numbers and other field extensions
anchor.snippet: The set of complex numbers C , numbers that can be written in the form

annotations.note: Field extensions are encoded via `Algebra K L` (which entails `Module K L`); `Complex.instAlgebra` provides ℂ as an ℝ-algebra.

annotations.label: Function spaces

anchor.snippet: Functions from any fixed set Ω to a field F also form vector spaces

annotations.note: `Pi.module` gives functions `Ω → F` a `Module F` structure pointwise.

anchors.snippet: Functions from any fixed set Ω to a field F also form vector spaces

annotations.label: Solutions of homogeneous linear equations

anchor.snippet: Systems of homogeneous linear equations are closely tied to vector spaces

annotations.note: Solutions to a homogeneous system are the kernel of the associated linear map, available as `LinearMap.ker` (a `Submodule`).

anchors.snippet: Systems of homogeneous linear equations are closely tied to vector spaces

annotations.label: Linear map

anchor.snippet: The relation of two vector spaces can be expressed by linear map or linear transformation

annotations.note: `LinearMap` is the structure of additive maps that commute with scalar multiplication.

anchors.snippet: The relation of two vector spaces can be expressed by linear map or linear transformation

annotations.label: Isomorphism of vector spaces

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism is a linear map f : V → W such that there exists an inverse map

annotations.note: `LinearEquiv` is a bundled invertible linear map, i.e. a linear isomorphism.

annotations.label: Space of linear maps and dual space

anchor.snippet: Linear maps V → W between two vector spaces form a vector space

annotations.note: `Module.Dual R M := M →ₗ[R] R`; the space of linear maps `V →ₗ[R] W` has a `Module` instance via `LinearMap.module`.

annotations.label: Classification of vector spaces by dimension

anchor.snippet: two vector spaces over a given field are isomorphic if their dimensions agree and vice versa

annotations.note: `nonempty_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq` produces a linear equivalence between two vector spaces with equal finrank.

annotations.label: Matrix representation of a linear map

anchor.snippet: Matrices are a useful notion to encode linear maps

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix` is the linear equivalence between linear maps and matrices given chosen bases.

anchors.snippet: Matrices are a useful notion to encode linear maps

annotations.label: Determinant detects isomorphism

anchor.snippet: The determinant det ( A ) of a square matrix A is a scalar that tells whether the associated map is an isomorphism or not

annotations.note: Over a field, `Matrix.det_ne_zero_iff_isUnit`/`LinearMap.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det` show invertibility is detected by the determinant.

annotations.label: Eigenvector and eigenvalue

anchor.snippet: Any nonzero vector v satisfying λ v = f ( v ) , where λ is a scalar, is called an eigenvector of f with eigenvalue λ

annotations.note: `Module.End.HasEigenvector` and `Module.End.HasEigenvalue` define these notions for endomorphisms of a module.

anchors.snippet: Any nonzero vector v satisfying λ v = f ( v ) , where λ is a scalar, is called an eigenvector of f with eigenvalue λ

annotations.label: Characteristic polynomial

anchor.snippet: the expression on the left hand side can be seen to be a polynomial function in λ , called the characteristic polynomial of f

annotations.note: `LinearMap.charpoly f` is defined as `det (X - f)` via a chosen basis; `Matrix.charpoly` is the matrix version.

anchors.snippet: the expression on the left hand side can be seen to be a polynomial function in λ , called the characteristic polynomial of f

annotations.label: Eigenspace

anchor.snippet: The set of all eigenvectors corresponding to a particular eigenvalue of f forms a vector space known as the eigenspace

annotations.note: `Module.End.eigenspace f μ` is the submodule of vectors satisfying `f x = μ • x`.

anchors.snippet: The set of all eigenvectors corresponding to a particular eigenvalue of f forms a vector space known as the eigenspace

annotations.label: Hyperplane

anchor.snippet: any subspace of dimension 1 less, i.e., of dimension

annotations.note: No dedicated `Hyperplane` definition was found; the notion appears only in informal docstrings and theorem names.

annotations.label: Quotient vector space

anchor.snippet: The counterpart to subspaces are quotient vector spaces

annotations.note: The quotient `M ⧸ S` of a module by a submodule (and its `Module` structure) is built via `Submodule.quotientRel` in `LinearAlgebra/Quotient/Defs.lean`.

anchors.snippet: The counterpart to subspaces are quotient vector spaces

annotations.label: Kernel of a linear map

anchor.snippet: The kernel

annotations.note: `LinearMap.ker f` is the submodule of vectors mapped to zero.

annotations.label: Linear differential operator

anchor.snippet: this assignment is linear, called a linear differential operator

annotations.note: Mathlib has linear maps and Fréchet derivatives but no abstract class called `LinearDifferentialOperator`; instances like `fderiv` realize the concept.

anchors.snippet: this assignment is linear, called a linear differential operator

annotations.label: Category of vector spaces is abelian

anchor.snippet: the category of vector spaces (over a fixed field

annotations.note: `ModuleCat R` is proved abelian for any ring `R`, in particular for fields.

anchors.snippet: the category of vector spaces (over a fixed field

annotations.label: Direct product of vector spaces

anchor.snippet: The direct product

annotations.note: The direct product `∏ i, M i` carries a `Module` instance via `Pi.module`.

annotations.label: Direct sum of vector spaces

anchor.snippet: A variant of this construction is the direct sum

annotations.note: `DirectSum ι (β i)` (notation `⨁ i, β i`) provides the direct sum of an indexed family of modules.

annotations.label: Bilinear map

anchor.snippet: is called bilinear if

annotations.note: Bilinear maps are represented as iterated linear maps `M →ₗ[R] N →ₗ[R] P`, constructed via `LinearMap.mk₂`.

annotations.label: Tensor product of vector spaces

anchor.snippet: The tensor product is a particular vector space that is a universal recipient of bilinear maps

annotations.note: `TensorProduct R M N` defines the tensor product of two modules.

anchors.snippet: The tensor product is a particular vector space that is a universal recipient of bilinear maps

annotations.label: Universal property of tensor product

anchor.snippet: This is called the universal property of the tensor product

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.lift` produces the unique linear map from a bilinear map, realising the universal property.

anchors.snippet: This is called the universal property of the tensor product

annotations.label: Ordered vector space

anchor.snippet: A vector space may be given a partial order

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes ordered modules via mixin classes such as `PosSMulStrictMono` over an ordered scalar action.

anchors.snippet: A vector space may be given a partial order

annotations.label: Normed and inner product spaces

anchor.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchor.snippet: Vector spaces endowed with such data are known as normed vector spaces and inner product spaces

annotations.note: `NormedSpace` and `InnerProductSpace` (in `Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Defs`) capture these structures.

annotations.label: Standard dot product

anchor.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchor.snippet: can be equipped with the standard dot product

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace 𝕜 ι` carries the standard dot product via its `InnerProductSpace` instance.

anchors.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchors.snippet: can be equipped with the standard dot product

annotations.label: Orthogonal vectors

anchor.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchor.snippet: are called orthogonal

annotations.note: Orthogonality is `inner x y = 0`; `Submodule.orthogonal` gives the orthogonal complement of a subspace.

anchors.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchors.snippet: are called orthogonal

annotations.label: Lorentz product on Minkowski space

anchor.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchor.snippet: An important variant of the standard dot product is used in Minkowski space

annotations.note: No formalisation of Minkowski space or its Lorentz bilinear form was found in Mathlib.

anchors.section: Normed vector spaces and inner product spaces
anchors.snippet: An important variant of the standard dot product is used in Minkowski space

annotations.label: Topological vector space

anchor.snippet: Convergence questions are treated by considering vector spaces

annotations.note: A topological vector space is a `Module` with `IsTopologicalAddGroup` plus `ContinuousSMul`; the classes live in `Mathlib/Topology/Algebra`.

annotations.label: Complete vector space

anchor.snippet: such a vector space is called complete

annotations.note: `CompleteSpace` from uniform-space theory specialises to completeness of a topological vector space.

anchors.snippet: such a vector space is called complete

annotations.label: Weierstrass approximation theorem

anchor.snippet: any continuous function on

annotations.note: `polynomialFunctions_closure_eq_top` states polynomials are uniformly dense in `C([a,b], ℝ)`.

anchors.snippet: any continuous function on

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem

anchor.snippet: The fundamental Hahn–Banach theorem is concerned with separating subspaces of appropriate topological vector spaces by continuous functionals

annotations.note: `exists_extension_norm_eq` is the analytic Hahn–Banach extension theorem; geometric versions live in `Mathlib/Analysis/LocallyConvex/Separation.lean`.

annotations.label: Banach space

anchor.snippet: Banach spaces , introduced by Stefan Banach , are complete normed vector spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses Banach spaces as `[NormedSpace 𝕜 E] [CompleteSpace E]`; no separate `Banach` class is needed.

annotations.label: Lebesgue spaces

anchor.snippet: and equipped with this norm are called Lebesgue spaces

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.Lp E p μ` defines Lᵖ as a normed space of equivalence classes of measurable functions.

annotations.label: Completeness of Lebesgue spaces

anchor.snippet: These spaces are complete

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.Lp.instCompleteSpace` provides the `CompleteSpace` instance on `Lᵖ`.

anchors.snippet: These spaces are complete

annotations.label: Sobolev spaces

anchor.snippet: Imposing boundedness conditions not only on the function, but also on its derivatives leads to Sobolev spaces

annotations.note: Sobolev norms appear (e.g. in `SobolevInequality.lean` and `Distribution/Sobolev.lean`), but no bundled `SobolevSpace` definition with `Wᵏ,ᵖ`-norm is present.

annotations.label: Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: Complete inner product spaces are known as Hilbert spaces , in honor of David Hilbert

annotations.note: Hilbert spaces are written `[InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E] [CompleteSpace E]`; no separate class is introduced.

anchors.snippet: Complete inner product spaces are known as Hilbert spaces , in honor of David Hilbert

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem

anchor.snippet: By the Stone–Weierstrass theorem , every continuous function on

annotations.note: The Stone–Weierstrass theorem is stated for point-separating subalgebras of `C(X, ℝ)` in `StoneWeierstrass.lean`.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem

anchor.snippet: The spectral theorem decomposes a linear compact operator acting on functions in terms of these eigenfunctions and their eigenvalues

annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite-dimensional spectral theorem (`LinearMap.IsSymmetric.eigenvectorBasis_apply_self_apply`) but not the full compact-operator version.

annotations.label: Algebra over a field

anchor.snippet: A vector space equipped with an additional bilinear operator defining the multiplication of two vectors is an algebra over a field

annotations.note: `Algebra R A` packages a commutative-ring-scalar action with multiplicative compatibility; over a field it is an algebra over a field.

annotations.label: Polynomial ring

anchor.snippet: forms an algebra known as the polynomial ring

annotations.note: `Polynomial R` is the (univariate) polynomial ring; it carries an `Algebra R R[X]` instance.

annotations.label: Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: Another crucial example are Lie algebras , which are neither commutative nor associative

annotations.note: `LieRing` and `LieAlgebra` formalise Lie algebras over a commutative ring.

annotations.label: Tensor algebra

anchor.snippet: is a formal way of adding products to any vector space

annotations.note: `TensorAlgebra R M` is the universal (associative) algebra generated by a module.

anchors.snippet: is a formal way of adding products to any vector space

annotations.label: Symmetric and exterior algebras

anchor.snippet: Forcing two such elements to be equal leads to the symmetric algebra , whereas forcing

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra R M` and `SymmetricAlgebra`/`IsSymmetricAlgebra` (`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/SymmetricAlgebra/Basic.lean`) formalise these constructions.

anchors.snippet: Forcing two such elements to be equal leads to the symmetric algebra , whereas forcing

annotations.label: Vector bundle

anchor.snippet: A vector bundle is a family of vector spaces parametrized continuously by a topological space X

annotations.note: `VectorBundle` is the typeclass capturing a fiber bundle with vector-space fibers and linear transition functions.

anchors.snippet: A vector bundle is a family of vector spaces parametrized continuously by a topological space X

annotations.label: Line bundle

anchor.snippet: The case dim V = 1 is called a line bundle

annotations.note: No dedicated `LineBundle` definition was found; only informal references appear in module docs.

anchors.snippet: The case dim V = 1 is called a line bundle

annotations.label: Tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: the tangent bundle consists of the collection of tangent spaces parametrized by the points of a differentiable manifold

annotations.note: `TangentBundle I M` is defined as the total space of `TangentSpace I` and instantiates `VectorBundle`.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: by the hairy ball theorem , there is no (tangent) vector field on the 2-sphere

annotations.note: A search for `hairy_ball`/`hairyBall` found no matches in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cotangent bundle

anchor.snippet: The cotangent bundle of a differentiable manifold consists, at every point of the manifold, of the dual of the tangent space

annotations.note: Mathlib has `CotangentSpace` in algebraic settings (e.g. Kähler differentials) but no smooth-manifold cotangent bundle.

annotations.label: Module over a ring

anchor.snippet: Modules are to rings what vector spaces are to fields

annotations.note: `Module R M` is the central definition of modules over a (semi)ring.

annotations.label: Free module

anchor.snippet: those modules that do (including all vector spaces) are known as free modules

annotations.note: `Module.Free R M` asserts the existence of a basis.

annotations.label: Affine space

anchor.snippet: an affine space is a set with a free transitive vector space action

annotations.note: Affine spaces are scoped notation `AffineSpace` for `AddTorsor G P` (a free transitive additive group action).

anchors.snippet: an affine space is a set with a free transitive vector space action

annotations.label: Affine subspace

anchor.snippet: an affine subspace is a subset of W obtained by translating a linear subspace V by a fixed vector

annotations.note: `AffineSubspace k P` is a subset closed under affine combinations, with `direction` the underlying linear subspace.

anchors.snippet: an affine subspace is a subset of W obtained by translating a linear subspace V by a fixed vector

annotations.label: Projective space

anchor.snippet: The set of one-dimensional subspaces of a fixed finite-dimensional vector space V is known as projective space

annotations.note: `Projectivization K V` is the quotient of nonzero vectors by scalar equivalence, i.e. projective space.
